title: Light - Reflection and Refraction
subject: Science
learningOutcomes: Understand Snell's Law of Refraction: $n_1 \sin i = n_2 \sin r$.
Simulate light ray bending when traveling from rarer (Air) to denser (Glass) medium.

objectives.text: Set incident angle $i=40^\circ$ and observe angle of refraction $r$.
Increase glass refractive index $n_2$ and observe greater ray bending.

theory: Snell's Law state that the ratio of sine of angle of incidence to sine of angle of refraction is constant for a given pair of media: $\frac{\sin i}{\sin r} = n_{21}$.
hints: Adjust the Incident Angle slider to see light ray bending in real time.
Observe the normal line (dashed line) perpendicular to the interface.
observationPrompts: Why does a light ray bend towards the normal when passing from air into glass?

realWorldExamples.title: Eyeglass Lenses
realWorldExamples.description: Optometrists design corrective lenses using refractive index and focal length calculations.

commonMistakes: Measuring angles from the glass surface rather than from the normal line.
